findings.description: BFS enqueues neighbors unconditionally (`seen` is written but never checked), so on cyclic graphs — which the docstring explicitly claims to support — the loop never terminates and the deque grows until OOM, and even on diamond-shaped DAGs work is exponential in path count; fix with `if neighbor not in seen: seen.add(neighbor); queue.append(neighbor)` for O(V+E).
count_paths recursively recomputes the count of every shared descendant with no memoization — O(2^n) on diamond-chain DAGs versus O(V+E) with a per-call {node: count} memo or a topological-order DP.

Unbounded recursion raises RecursionError on any cycle (the class accepts cyclic edges) or any path deeper than ~1000 frames, which long pipeline chains can hit — an iterative topo-order DP fixes this and the memoization issue together; detail deferred to critic-logic.

Reads of missing keys on the defaultdict (lines 30/40/54/61) silently insert empty lists, so nominally read-only methods mutate state — wasting up to O(V) dict entries on sink-node queries and breaking concurrent-reader safety; use `self._edges.get(node, ())` on read paths.

add_edge appends without dedup, so idempotent re-registration (common in pipeline reload loops) grows edge lists without bound and inflates memory plus every traversal — dedupe or document parallel-edge semantics.
logger.info per registered node makes bulk graph loads (millions of nodes) emit millions of log records on a hot ingestion path — drop to DEBUG or log aggregate counts.
neighbors() allocates a fresh O(degree) list copy per call — defensively correct, only worth changing if profiling shows it hot; micro-optimization nit.
reachable's result includes `start` only when it lies on a cycle and excludes it otherwise — inconsistent semantic contract; deferred to critic-logic.
